title: AI can push your Stream Deck buttons for you
summary: AI assistants can now automate interactions with Stream Deck devices, potentially reducing the need for manual input in certain workflows.

title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5 with Autonomous Agent Capabilities
summary: GPT-5 can now autonomously complete multi-step work tasks, significantly increasing AI's ability to replace routine knowledge work.

title: The Download: AI health tools and the Pentagon’s Anthropic culture war
summary: The increasing development of AI health tools by major tech companies suggests a growing trend of AI integration into the healthcare sector, potentially leading to job displacement in that field.

title: EU Passes AI Workplace Protection Act Requiring Human Oversight
summary: New EU regulation mandates human oversight for AI systems used in hiring and workplace decisions, slowing automated replacement.

title: All the latest in AI ‘music’
summary: The article highlights challenges and ethical concerns around AI-generated music, suggesting potential delays in its widespread adoption and impact on human jobs.

title: Google Automates 40% of Internal Code Review with AI
summary: Google reports that AI now handles 40% of code reviews internally, reducing the need for junior developer roles in QA.

title: Bluesky’s new app is an AI for customizing your feed
summary: An AI tool that allows users to customize their social media feed may contribute to the broader adoption of AI in personal and professional workflows, potentially accelerating AI's role in replacing certain job functions.

title: Stanford Study: AI Creates 1.2 Jobs for Every Job It Displaces
summary: New research suggests AI is creating new job categories faster than it eliminates existing ones, particularly in AI oversight roles.

title: Amazon Replaces 2,000 Customer Service Roles with AI Chatbots
summary: Amazon transitions customer service operations to AI, affecting 2,000 positions across North American fulfillment centers.

title: Anthropic's Claude Passes Bar Exam with Top 1% Score
summary: Claude achieves a top 1% score on the bar examination, raising questions about the future of legal research and junior associate roles.
